title: Cough red-flag screening documentation

red_flags.text: Lists cough features that require serious-condition assessment or face-to-face escalation.
red_flags.rationale: Supports clinician-assessed cough warning prompts for cough red-flag screening; no decision is generated.
red_flags.evidence_extract: Lists cough features that require serious-condition assessment or face-to-face escalation.

source.title: DHA Telehealth Clinical Guidelines for Virtual Management of Cough – 46

red_flags.text: Supports clinician-led emergency, family-medicine, and pulmonary referral according to the documented cough context.
red_flags.rationale: Supports qualified referral context for cough red-flag screening; no decision is generated.
red_flags.evidence_extract: Supports clinician-led emergency, family-medicine, and pulmonary referral according to the documented cough context.

source.title: DHA Telehealth Clinical Guidelines for Virtual Management of Cough – 46

investigations.text: Supports chest radiography, spirometry with reversibility, sputum testing where infection is suspected, selected inflammatory markers, specialist referral for persistent or unclear cough, and upper-airway specialist assessment when indicated.
investigations.rationale: Supports adult chronic-cough investigation and referral context for cough red-flag screening; no decision is generated.
investigations.evidence_extract: Supports chest radiography, spirometry with reversibility, sputum testing where infection is suspected, selected inflammatory markers, specialist referral for persistent or unclear cough, and upper-airway specialist assessment when indicated.

source.title: British Thoracic Society Clinical Statement on chronic cough in adults

source_limitations: All item-level mappings remain unsupported pending separate clinician review and signed approval.
Duration, haemoptysis, breathlessness, systemic symptoms, cancer or infection risk, oxygenation, examination, tests, risk formulation, urgency and plan remain absent.
No diagnosis, risk conclusion, investigation, treatment, medicine, dose, referral, escalation or disposition is generated for cough red-flag screening.
Any legacy item not explicitly mapped to an exact section remains unsupported pending qualified clinician review.
No mapping authorises an automatic diagnosis, differential, investigation, management decision, medicine, dose, procedure, referral, urgency conclusion, discharge decision, or patient instruction.
curation_policy: Direct guideline curation: retain only explicitly mapped content; rewrite only with explicit item mapping; remove unmapped legacy content; add only committed exact-section evidence summaries.
